findings.description: Ensure image alternative is not repeated as text
findings.evidence: Fix all of the following: Element contains element with alt text that duplicates existing text [

findings.description: Repeated page header/footer content in HTML
findings.evidence: The following text blocks repeat 3+ times, likely page headers/footers that should not appear in the body: 'process components evaluation criteria' (5x); 'process components water use' (3x).

findings.description: Flow Regime Changes bar chart (Stream Condition Middle Deep River, Node 280) is missing from the HTML conversion
findings.evidence: PDF page 15 shows a grouped bar chart titled 'Stream Condition Middle Deep River (Node 280) June - November' with bars for Unimpaired, 2003 Demands, 2030 Demands, and 2050 Demands across QAA percentage ranges. The HTML shows only the text headings 'Flow Regime Changes' and 'Stream Condition Middle Deep River (Node 280) June - November' but the chart image itself is absent.

findings.description: Venn diagram showing project organization relationships is missing from the HTML conversion
findings.evidence: PDF page 19 shows a Venn diagram with three overlapping circles labeled 'Data Collection HDR', 'Model Development HydroLogics', and 'Data, Model Evaluation & Plan Development NCDWR'. The HTML renders this only as a flat bullet list of the three items, with the Venn diagram graphic absent.

findings.description: The Flow Regime Changes bar chart from PDF page 15 is missing from the HTML. The PDF contains a substantive stacked bar chart showing 'Stream Condition Middle Deep River (Node 280) June - November' with data for Unimpaired, 2003, 2030, and 2050 Demands across multiple flow-level categories. The HTML only has the text labels but no corresponding tag for the chart.
findings.evidence:
PDF page 15 shows a detailed bar chart with legend entries (< 10% of QAA*, 10-20% of QAA, etc.) and four scenario columns. HTML section for Flow Regime Changes contains only: '

Stream Condition Middle Deep River (Node 280)
June - November

' with no tag.

findings.description: The Water Supply Deficits table from PDF page 16 appears twice in the HTML: once as an embedded image and once as a fully rendered HTML <table>. The PDF contains this data only once.
findings.evidence: HTML contains both '' AND a full '' element with identical data rows (Orange-Alamance, Hillsborough, Piedmont Minerals, Raleigh, Durham, SGWASA) immediately after the image.
